title: Afrafzelia attenuata
intro: Afrafzelia attenuata, also known as the West African kevazingo tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Leguminosae. The plant has been traditionally used in African medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Afrafzelia attenuata include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from the plant include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote wound healing.
